differential_diagnoses: Meconium aspiration syndrome with secondary infection
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn
next_steps: Obtain high-resolution chest computed tomography (HRCT) with contrast to better define pulmonary architecture and vascular supply
Perform flexible bronchoscopy to evaluate for airway compression, endobronchial obstruction, or anatomical anomalies
Obtain blood and tracheal aspirate cultures to guide antibiotic de-escalation or optimization
Consult pediatric thoracic surgery for potential surgical intervention if a malformation is confirmed
Continue optimize management of pulmonary hypertension and ventilatory support
Obtain high-resolution chest computed tomography (HRCT) with contrast to define anatomy and vascular supply
Perform flexible bronchoscopy to evaluate for anatomical obstruction or malacia
Obtain tracheal aspirate for Ureaplasma PCR and comprehensive microbiological cultures
Consult pediatric thoracic surgery for potential diagnostic and therapeutic intervention
Perform a formal swallow study to rule out chronic aspiration contributing to persistent opacity
Review maternal prenatal and placental pathology records for evidence of fetal systemic infection
